comment_text.sn35.162:1.1: For Mahākoṭṭhita, see note on SN 22.122:1.1. | This discourse parallels SN 22.137.

root_text.sn35.162:1.1: Atha kho āyasmā mahākoṭṭhiko yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami …pe… ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā koṭṭhiko bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.162:1.2: “sādhu me, bhante, bhagavā saṅkhittena dhammaṁ desetu, yamahaṁ bhagavato dhammaṁ sutvā eko vūpakaṭṭho appamatto ātāpī pahitatto vihareyyan”ti.
root_text.sn35.162:2.1: “Yaṁ kho, koṭṭhika, aniccaṁ tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.162:2.3: Cakkhu kho, koṭṭhika, aniccaṁ; tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.162:2.4: Rūpā aniccā; tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.162:2.5: Cakkhuviññāṇaṁ aniccaṁ; tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.162:2.6: Cakkhusamphasso anicco; tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.162:2.7: Yampidaṁ cakkhusamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi aniccaṁ; tatra te chando pahātabbo …pe…
root_text.sn35.162:2.8: jivhā aniccā; tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.162:2.9: Rasā aniccā; tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.162:2.10: Jivhāviññāṇaṁ aniccaṁ; tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.162:2.11: Jivhāsamphasso anicco; tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.162:2.12: Yampidaṁ jivhāsamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi aniccaṁ; tatra te chando pahātabbo …pe…
root_text.sn35.162:2.13: mano anicco; tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.162:2.14: Dhammā aniccā; tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.162:2.15: Manoviññāṇaṁ aniccaṁ; tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.162:2.16: Manosamphasso anicco; tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.162:2.17: Yampidaṁ manosamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi aniccaṁ; tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.162:2.18: Yaṁ kho, koṭṭhika, aniccaṁ tatra te chando pahātabbo”ti.

translation_text.sn35.162:1.1: Then Venerable Mahākoṭṭhita went up to the Buddha … and asked him,
translation_text.sn35.162:1.2: “Sir, may the Buddha please teach me Dhamma in brief. When I’ve heard it, I’ll live alone, withdrawn, diligent, keen, and resolute.”
translation_text.sn35.162:2.1: “Koṭṭhita, you should give up desire for what is impermanent.
translation_text.sn35.162:2.6: and eye contact are impermanent: you should give up desire for them.
translation_text.sn35.162:2.7: The pleasant, painful, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on eye contact is also impermanent: you should give up desire for it.
translation_text.sn35.162:2.16: and mind contact are impermanent: you should give up desire for them.
translation_text.sn35.162:2.17: The pleasant, painful, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on mind contact is also impermanent: you should give up desire for it.
translation_text.sn35.162:2.18: Koṭṭhita, you should give up desire for what is impermanent.”
